chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يَجُوزُ لِلْمُحْرِمَةِ أَنْ تَلْبَسَهُ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ وَ الْحُلِيِّ وَ مَا يُكْرَهُ لَهَا مِنْ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 85 – What is allowed for the female in Ihraam to wear from the clothes, and the ornaments, and what is disliked for her from that

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/292)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/292)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا يَحِلُّ لِلْمَرْأَةِ أَنْ تَلْبَسَ وَ هِيَ مُحْرِمَةٌ قَالَ الثِّيَابُ كُلُّهَا مَا خَلَا الْقُفَّازَيْنِ وَ الْبُرْقُعَ وَ الْحَرِيرَ قُلْتُ تَلْبَسُ الْخَزَّ قَالَ نَعَمْ قُلْتُ فَإِنَّ سَدَاهُ الْإِبْرِيسَمُ وَ هُوَ حَرِيرٌ قَالَ مَا لَمْ يَكُنْ حَرِيراً خَالِصاً فَلَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, or someone else from Dawood Bin Al Husayn, from Abu Uyayna who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws, ‘What is Permissible for the woman to wear while she is in Ihraam?’ Heasws said: ‘The clothes, all of them, apart from the two gloves, and the veil (Niqaab/Burqa), and the silk’. I said, ‘Sha can wear the fur (Al-Khazz)?’ Heasws said: ‘Yes’. I said, ‘Supposing its lacing is of silk and it is (also of) silk?’ As long as it does not happen to be purely silk, so there is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 6. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad Ahmad ibn Muhammad from and others from Dawud ibn al-Husayn from abu ‘Uyaynah who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about what can a woman wear in the state of Ihram. He (the Imam) said, ‘She can wear all kinds of clothes except al-Qafazin, veil and silk. 5 I then asked, ‘Can she wear al-Khazz (a certain kind of fabric), with its warp made of silk?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘As long as it is not pure silk, it is permissible.”’
